Acceptance: Magician can choose a wild-suit Disc variant; Strength can apply up to two Disc actions and can skip the intermediate piece size or territory value when both actions grow the same target; Star performs the orient-minion step before Disc and may source territory-growth replacement cards from anywhere in the discard pile, including the just-discarded Star when played from hand; Sun can apply Cup then Disc in rule order and can skip the intermediate step when both powers affect the same newly-created target; all variants preserve explicit success/error maps, history, discard behavior, and schema/deck invariants.
close_reason: done

title: Add Coins/Discs gameplay feature coverage and docs
description: Add Gherkin-style and focused unit coverage for the completed Coins/Discs rule slice, and update docs that describe implemented gameplay behavior. Acceptance: feature scenarios cover piece growth, territory growth, owned versus enemy orientation rules, missing stash rejection, large-piece rejection, missing replacement-card rejection, enemy-occupied territory rejection, hand-source discard behavior, Star discard-pile replacement behavior, and Strength or Sun shortcut behavior where implemented. README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the implemented move surface, tests, or verification workflow changes; clojure -M:test passes.
close_reason: Added Disc Gherkin coverage and synchronized docs; clojure -M:test passes

title: Draw/discard move panel cannot select discards at a full hand
description: README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md describe the standalone draw/discard turn as calling apply-draw-move, including optional discard-card-ids and drawing after selected discards. The browser move-selection state only requires draw-count, has no event/control path for selecting discard-card-ids, and max-draw-count ignores cards the player could discard. Result: at the normal six-card hand, the Draw cards source is disabled, so the UI cannot perform the basic rules action to discard any or none and then draw. Evidence: README.md lines 43, 45, and 61. AGENTS.md lines 35 and 47. src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc move-source-definitions, max-draw-count, draw-count-options, and move-command. Add discard-card selection state, controls, command building, and tests, or narrow the docs if this remains intentionally pure-only.
close_reason: Implemented draw discard-card selection in the move panel

title: Split remaining monolithic app-state and game-state tests by subsystem
description: bd-1eln moved shared fixture builders into test support, but the main behavioral tests remain very large: wc -l reports 5324 lines for test/gnostica/app_state_test.clj and 5015 lines for test/gnostica/game_state_test.clj. That makes fresh review difficult and lets move-selection, app-db, and gameplay transition regressions hide in broad files. Acceptance: split the remaining tests into focused namespaces by subsystem or move family, especially move-selection controls/targets/commands/registry and game-state move families; keep clojure -M:test running all new namespaces; preserve or improve fixture reuse without changing production behavior.
close_reason: Split app-state and game-state behavioral tests into focused namespaces with shared test support; clojure -M:test passes.

title: Keep Sun Cup target-piece controls visible after Disc mode selection
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs:824 hides the Cup target-piece chooser once :sun-disc-mode is set. Sun can Cup onto a piece and then skip or perform a Disc follow-up, but after choosing the Disc mode the original Cup target-piece selection disappears and the remaining target-piece control belongs to the Disc step. Acceptance: keep a distinct Cup target-piece summary/control visible throughout the Sun flow, keep Disc target controls separate, and add view-model or UI coverage for piece Cup target plus skip and piece Cup target plus Disc piece.
close_reason: Fixed Sun Cup target-piece summary during Disc staging

title: Split move-selection state selectors from power semantics
description: Fresh-eye review found src/main/gnostica/move_selection/state.cljc named as state selectors but also owning card-power semantics and move-family classification. Evidence: lines 11-143 are ordinary app-db selectors and source-command helpers; lines 145-276 derive selected power, World copy state, Fool play state, and major source opts; lines 299-489 encode composite major sequencing, sword major sequencing, suit variant detection, replacement value math, and manipulation-power predicates. Problem: query helpers, card taxonomy interpretation, command-source shaping, and per-power business predicates are coupled under a generic state namespace. That makes it hard to tell whether a change belongs in power_taxonomy, registry, flow, commands, or state. Acceptance: split simple app-db selectors from active-power and per-power capability derivation, move taxonomy-like helpers to an appropriate browser-free taxonomy or move-selection power context namespace, keep public facade behavior stable, and add focused tests for World, Fool, composite major, suit variant, and replacement-value derivations.
close_reason: Split move-selection power context from state selectors and covered World, Fool, composite, suit variant, and replacement-value derivations.

title: Keep unavailable turn actions visible but disabled
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/header.cljs:65 wraps the entire turn-action group in (when can-end-turn?). During the initial no-piece placement state, game-state/app-state report that ending the turn is unavailable, so the End turn and Challenge controls disappear instead of staying visible but disabled. AGENTS.md says header end-turn and challenge buttons should stay disabled while a no-piece current player still owes initial placement. Acceptance: render the turn-action group for active games/current players, disable End turn when can-end-turn? is false, keep Challenge disabled when can-announce-challenge? is false, and add header/view-model coverage for the initial-placement-blocked state.
close_reason: Kept header turn actions visible for active games, disabled End turn and Challenge independently, added initial-placement-blocked view-model coverage, and verified with clojure -M:test, clojure -M:lint, and clojure -M:release.

title: Help dialogs need modal focus and shortcut containment
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/help.cljs renders the hotkey and icon help overlays with role=dialog and aria-modal=true, but opening them does not move focus into the dialog, trap Tab/Shift+Tab, restore focus to the opener, or make the header/board/panels inert. src/main/gnostica/app/keyboard.cljs also keeps global shortcuts live while the dialog is open, so keys can affect the background UI. Add real modal focus management plus smoke coverage for Tab cycling, Escape, and focus restoration.
close_reason: Fixed modal focus, shortcut containment, and smoke coverage

title: First-piece placement click does not work on wastelands
description: Observed: In the first-piece placement flow without dragging, clicking an empty territory target works and the small piece appears. Clicking an empty wasteland target does not place the piece. Expected: Legal empty wasteland click targets should behave like territory click targets for initial placement, matching keyboard/drag validation and the pure apply-initial-placement support for :wasteland targets. Context: Reported after verifying territory clicks work; the issue appears specific to wasteland click handling rather than pure game-state rules.
close_reason: Fixed CSS and Three.js active wasteland target clicks for staged first-piece placement; added CSS wasteland-click smoke coverage; verified with clojure -M:lint, clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke.

title: Document pending-move-tray-view as a public UI subscription
description: README.md and AGENTS.md list the public composed view-model subscriptions consumed by the UI, but omit :gnostica.app/pending-move-tray-view. Code marks it public in src/main/gnostica/app/ids.cljc and app.cljs plus ui/move_panel/pending_tray.cljs subscribe to it directly; MIND_MAP.md also implies the pending tray is rendered from move-panel-view. Align the docs so pending-tray ownership and the subscription boundary are clear.
close_reason: Documented pending-move-tray-view as a public composed UI subscription in README, AGENTS, and mind map node 24.

title: Separate smoke runner CDP harness from Gnostica assertions
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: test/gnostica/smoke_runner.clj is 1125 lines and combines Chrome process management, DevTools protocol transport, JS snippets, screenshot analysis, input dispatch, server startup, and Gnostica board assertions. The Gnostica-specific scenario in run-happy-viewport! around lines 929-1032 sits on top of a reusable browser harness, but both layers are in one namespace.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract CDP/Chrome lifecycle, command/evaluate/wait/input helpers into a test support namespace.
- Move page-stat JavaScript snippets and readiness predicates into focused Gnostica smoke modules or data structures.
- Leave smoke_runner.clj as high-level scenario orchestration.
- Preserve current clojure -M:smoke behavior and diagnostics.
close_reason: done

title: Harden pinned CDN script loading for Three.js
description:
Problem: `src/main/resources/index.html` loads Three.js and OrbitControls from pinned jsDelivr URLs at runtime, but there is no subresource integrity, crossorigin policy, local fallback, or automated check that the fetched bytes still match the expected r128 global build. The app has a CSS fallback when globals are absent, but release reproducibility and offline/corporate-network behavior still depend on a third-party CDN.

Evidence:
- `index.html` references `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.128.0/...` directly before `/js/main.js`.
- `src/main/externs/three.ext.js` is tied to the r128 global API, so accidental CDN or add-on drift can compile successfully but fail at runtime.

Acceptance criteria:
- Choose a hardened loading strategy that preserves the npm-free workflow: SRI plus crossorigin, vendored pinned scripts, or a documented local fallback path.
- Verify Three.js and OrbitControls are on the same r128 release line and fail visibly if a mismatched global is loaded.
- Add or update an automated/smoke check that detects missing or mismatched CDN globals before user interaction.
- Document the chosen strategy in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
- `clojure -M:release` succeeds after the change with advanced-compilation externs still aligned.
close_reason: Added SRI/crossorigin to pinned Three.js CDN scripts, gated the runtime on r128 plus OrbitControls, added missing and mismatched global smoke coverage, and documented the strategy.

title: Show unselected orientation controls as none instead of Unknown
description: src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs:490 renders Current using pieces/orientation-label even when no orientation has been selected, and src/main/gnostica/pieces.cljc returns Unknown for nil. In move staging this reads like an error state rather than an unchosen optional value. Acceptance: unselected orientation controls display a neutral value such as None or Choose orientation while truly invalid orientation values can still surface as errors elsewhere.
close_reason: Implemented: unselected move-panel orientation controls now show None while invalid orientation labels still surface as Unknown. Verified with clojure -M:test and clojure -M:release.

title: Direct move-entry previews and orientation compass are not implemented
description: Direct move-entry spec and closed tickets bd-5h4.4 and bd-5h4.7 describe ghost previews, snapped Rod or Hermit path previews, distance and destination feedback, and a compass or ring for orientation. Current renderer preview is a text summary from three_board.cljs drag-preview-summary backed by pointer.cljs target descriptor status; no path, mutation preview, distance visual, or orientation compass/ring is implemented, and docs/direct_move_entry_spec.md still lists preview and compass work as an implementation phase. Acceptance: either implement the visual preview and compass behavior in 3D and CSS fallback with tests or smoke coverage, or explicitly mark it deferred and split remaining tickets so the direct manipulation epic status matches shipped behavior.
notes: Implemented shared move-preview data, CSS fallback spatial overlays, Three.js preview overlay, orientation compass callbacks, Disc/Sword/Sun mutation summaries, app-state tests, docs, and mind-map update. Verified with clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke.
close_reason: Implemented shared direct move previews and orientation compass rendering with tests and smoke verification.

title: Extract shared spatial and target helpers for game-state rules
description: Several pure rule namespaces define near-identical helpers for coordinates, target summaries, discard-pile lookups, and piece movement: coordinate-map appears in cup.cljc, rod.cljc, disc.cljc, sword.cljc, manipulation.cljc, and composite.cljc; same-coordinate? appears in rod/disc/sword/manipulation/composite and move_selection; discard-pile-card appears in major/cup/rod/disc/sword; move-piece-to-space appears in rod/manipulation/composite. The duplication makes subtle targeting or accounting differences hard to spot. Acceptance: add shared pure helper namespaces for spatial lookup/target summaries/source-card lookup or otherwise consolidate these helpers. Keep local variants only when the semantic difference is named and tested, and keep clojure -M:test passing.
close_reason: Consolidated shared spatial and discard-card helpers across pure game-state rules; added direct spatial helper tests; clojure -M:test and clojure-lsp diagnostics pass.

title: Clarify skipped Fool reveal disposition against rules text
description: docs/rules.txt:128-129 says Fool turns over the next draw-pile card and plays it, repeated once, with powers optional. features/draw_major_powers.feature:3-9 asserts Rose can reveal two cards without playing them and that both revealed cards end in the discard pile. test/gnostica/feature_world.clj:1113-1120 encodes this as empty reveal actions. Decide and document whether skipped Fool reveal cards are discarded, returned, left revealed, or otherwise handled, then update rules and Cucumber coverage to match.
close_reason: Clarified that skipped Fool reveal cards are discarded and added Cucumber event/discard coverage.

title: Shuffle the deck before dealing table cards
description:
Board setup currently deals the first nine cards from the ordered deck into the 3x3 territory grid. Add a shuffle step before dealing cards onto the table so each new table uses a randomized deck order.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shuffle the tarot deck before selecting the nine table/territory cards.
- Preserve existing board invariants: nine face-up cards, 3x3 placement, and alternating portrait/landscape orientations.
- Keep the shuffle path testable, preferably by injecting or seeding randomness instead of hard-coding global random state.
- Update or add tests that prove the deal no longer depends on the unshuffled deck order while the existing layout rules still hold.
close_reason: done

title: Fade board velvet into black beyond wastelands
description:
Adjust the board background so the velvet colour under and around territory cards fades to black with distance from the playable area. The velvet should remain visible under the territory cards and wasteland outlines, but by the time the surface extends past the wasteland squares and roughly halfway into the void it should be black.

Acceptance criteria:
- Three.js board rendering uses a distance-based fade or equivalent material/texture so the central territory area remains velvet and the outer void reaches black at the specified distance.
- CSS fallback board background matches the same visual intent closely enough that wasteland squares sit on velvet and farther void space reads as black.
- The fade respects current board-space/wasteland layout helpers instead of hard-coding only the initial 3x3 board dimensions where avoidable.
- Territory cards, wasteland outlines, pieces, card selection highlights, and icon popovers remain readable against the darker outer surface.
- Desktop and mobile smoke checks continue to see nonblank board content and dark table/void pixels; update smoke expectations if existing pixel thresholds assume uniform velvet.
- Update MIND_MAP.md or README.md if renderer behavior or smoke assertions change materially.
close_reason: Implemented the Three.js and CSS board fade, updated smoke checks and docs.

title: Reduce app-state facade re-export boilerplate
description: src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc and src/main/gnostica/app_state/moves.cljc carry long manual alias lists. app_state.cljc lines 110-208 mirror app_state/moves.cljc lines 8-110 for move-selection functions. The public facade is intentional, but the duplication is easy to forget when adding or removing APIs. Simplify with a small checked-in export table plus macro, or a narrower facade API grouped by feature, while keeping generated or public vars stable for callers. Acceptance: one source of truth drives the facade aliases, and static tests verify the intended public vars remain exported.
close_reason: Centralized app-state move facade aliases in a checked export table and macro; added public export and clj-kondo hook sync tests; verified clojure -M:test, clojure -M:lint, and clojure -M:release.

title: Tighten game schema invariants for impossible gameplay states
description: The Malli GameState schema currently accepts several states that the pure gameplay contract says should be impossible. Confirmed examples: a piece map with both :space-index and :space validates because Piece is an open :or map; a piece in {:space {:kind :wasteland :row 99 :col 99}} validates even when that coordinate is not a current wasteland; and [:setup :target-score] accepts any positive int such as 99 despite create-game allowing only 8, 9, or 10. Acceptance: require exactly one piece location shape, validate wasteland piece coordinates against board-layout/wasteland-spaces, restrict target score to game-state/allowed-target-scores, and surface specific invariant errors for these cases.
close_reason: Implemented schema invariants for piece locations, wasteland coordinates, and official target scores.

title: Restore clojure-lsp diagnostics after board drag-hover changes
description: A fresh clojure-lsp diagnostics run exits nonzero even though clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke pass. Current diagnostics report unresolved symbols in src/main/gnostica/ui/board.cljs around the r/with-let drag-hover/finally locals, plus unused bindings in app_state.cljc and move_selection.cljc. Acceptance: make clojure-lsp diagnostics exit zero again, either by rewriting the board-stage lifecycle shape so the analyzer sees the bindings, configuring an intentional analyzer limitation, and removing the real unused bindings; keep release and smoke green.
close_reason: Fixed clojure-lsp diagnostics; verified diagnostics, tests, release, and smoke

title: Map gesture pending alternatives to concrete missing choices
description: The pending gesture tray exposes raw move-selection requirements instead of the concrete choice the user must make. Evidence: move-selection can report internal missing fields such as :target-resolution, :sun-disc-target-piece-id, :sun-disc-target-board-index, and :sun-disc-replacement-card-id. gesture-intent/alternative-options handles :target-resolution by always returning orientation options and lets the Sun-specific fields fall through to empty options, so the tray can show a generic prompt with wrong or empty alternatives while Detailed entry has the real stage. Acceptance: derive pending-tray alternatives from the same concrete stage mapping used by move-selection, or add explicit alternatives for target-resolution and Sun-specific requirements; cover Cup territory, Cup wasteland, and Sun Disc modes.
close_reason: Mapped pending gesture alternatives to concrete choices with Cup and Sun coverage

title: Clarify smoke fixture modes across README, AGENTS, and mind map
description: The docs overgeneralize smoke fixture behavior. README.md and AGENTS.md say smoke fixture options bypass the lobby with deterministic players, deck order, and demo pieces; MIND_MAP.md node [42] narrows that to deterministic six-player setup, smoke board pieces, and deck order. src/main/gnostica/fixtures.cljc has at least two smoke modes: major-icons uses six players, smoke board pieces, and a deck order, while direct-drop uses the default two players, no demo pieces, open panels, and identity shuffle. Acceptance: the docs name the distinct smoke modes and avoid implying every smoke path has the major-icons six-player/demo-piece/deck-order setup.
close_reason: Documented distinct major-icons and direct-drop smoke fixture modes.

title: Direct move-entry smoke does not exercise real touch input
description: Closed direct manipulation verification ticket bd-5h4.9 asked for mobile/touch viewport coverage for at least one card drag and one piece drag. Current smoke runs a mobile viewport, but the gesture probes are mouse or synthetic only: browser dispatch-drag uses CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent, hand-card-drag-js and fallback-piece-drag-js dispatch DragEvent dragstart directly, and there is no touchStart, pointerType touch, or long-press path. This can pass while touch or pen direct gestures are broken. Acceptance: add a mobile touch or pointer smoke path, or document touch as deferred, that exercises at least one card and one piece gesture through real touch or pointer input and fails if pending tray or Detailed entry cannot be reached.
close_reason: Added mobile touch smoke coverage for hand-card and Three.js piece gestures

title: Clarify AGENTS test command comment includes feature scenarios
description: AGENTS.md:15 says clojure -M:test runs Clojure tests for shared code, while AGENTS.md:45 and README.md:91 say the same command also runs custom Gherkin-style gameplay feature scenarios. The command summary should not make feature failures look outside the normal test path. Acceptance: update the command comment to match README.md and test/gnostica/test_runner.clj.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md test command comment to include gameplay feature scenarios.

title: Add a real lint and formatting verification path
description: There is partial lint infrastructure but no runnable project check: deps.edn has no :lint alias, clj-kondo is not on PATH in this workspace, and .clj-kondo contains imported config/cache data without a documented command. A probe of clojure -M:lint just started a plain Clojure REPL, so it would not catch syntax, lint, or formatting drift. There is visible formatting drift in src/main/gnostica/app/events.cljs around select-move-territory-card-source, select-move-rod-mode, move-rod-mode-options, move-target-piece-options, and move-rod-orientation-required?, plus a tabbed branch in src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc reveal-lobby-bids. Acceptance: add a documented lint/format command or remove stale lint expectations; make the command fail on parse/lint/format errors across src and test while excluding generated src/main/resources/js; normalize existing formatting so future review noise is reduced.
close_reason: Added clojure -M:lint verifier with clj-kondo and cljfmt checks, documented the command, and normalized existing source formatting drift.

title: Implement Cup enemy-piece creation targets
description: docs/rules.txt Cups section allows a Cup to create an enemy small piece when targeting an enemy piece, preserving the enemy piece orientation. apply-cup-move only supports creating the current player's small piece on a target territory and its command target has no piece-target variant. README.md and AGENTS.md document the narrower behavior, so the current Cup slice omits a legal Cup branch. Acceptance: add explicit command/UI support and tests, or document the branch as unsupported in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
close_reason: Implemented Cup enemy-piece creation targets

title: Document app-state facade export table ownership
description:
`src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc` generates many public aliases through `def-facade-aliases` and `gnostica.app-state.facade-exports/app-state-move-alias-vars`, with a dedicated `facade_exports_test`. README.md and AGENTS.md send contributors to docs/architecture.md/MIND_MAP.md for app-state ownership, but those maps only mention db, lobby, gestures, moves, and view-model namespaces and omit the facade export table and macro. That makes public facade changes less discoverable and can lead to missing export or test updates.

Acceptance: document `facade-exports` and `facade-macros` ownership in docs/architecture.md and the compact app-state mind-map node without expanding README.
close_reason: done

title: Consolidate Disc and Sword target normalization helpers
description:
Fresh-eye review after the shared Disc/Sword source and replacement cleanup found another near-parallel target-normalization seam.

Evidence:
- src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc:54-60 and src/main/gnostica/game_state/sword.cljc:59-66 both wrap the same spatial target-coordinate check.
- Disc piece target normalization at disc.cljc:99-141 mirrors Sword piece target normalization at sword.cljc:146-215, with suit-specific damage and orientation rules layered into the same shape.
- Disc territory target normalization at disc.cljc:143-188 mirrors Sword territory target normalization beginning at sword.cljc:217, including target-cell lookup, source orientation checks, enemy occupancy checks, and structured target maps.
- Both files then carry similar resolve-target dispatch around disc.cljc:190-220 and sword.cljc:298 onward.

Problem: target legality, coordinate error data, and territory/piece target map construction can drift between growth and attack rules. The shared card-source helpers removed one duplication layer, but target normalization is still maintained twice.

Acceptance:
- Extract a shared suit-target resolver/helper for piece and territory targeting, parameterized by suit-specific labels, error codes/messages, target kinds, damage/orientation validation, and occupancy rules where they differ.
- Preserve Disc and Sword public behavior, history events, and structured error payloads unless an intentional wording change is documented.
- Keep Disc and Sword tests passing and add focused parity/regression coverage for target coordinate errors, enemy-occupied territory rejection, self-target behavior, and orientation validation.
close_reason: Implemented shared Disc/Sword suit target resolver with parity coverage

title: First-piece drag highlights every placement area instead of only hovered target
description:
Actual: While dragging the first small piece for initial placement, every legal empty territory and wasteland is highlighted at once.

Expected: During a first-piece drag, only the wasteland or territory that would be selected if the drag were released at the current pointer location should show the active drag highlight. Other legal destinations may remain eligible through descriptors, but they should not all render as highlighted drag targets.

Repro notes: Start a normal local game from the lobby so the current player has no pieces on the board. Begin dragging the first/initial placement piece over the board. All empty territories and wastelands highlight instead of just the current drop target.

Scope: Direct manipulation initial placement highlighting in both Three.js and CSS fallback paths. Keep legal-target descriptors available for hit testing, preserve pending tray/Detailed entry fallback behavior, and avoid mutating :game before confirmation.

Related: follow-up to bd-2vcc, which restored source-only first-piece drag target descriptors but made all legal destinations visibly highlighted.
close_reason: Fixed first-piece drag highlighting so only the hovered territory or wasteland renders as the active drag target while other legal destinations remain available for hit testing, with CSS and Three.js smoke coverage.

title: Render Gnostica icons on major arcana cards
description: Add Gnostica icon triplets to major arcana card rendering, using the Gnostica Stickers reference at https://www.wunderland.com/icehouse/GnosticaStickas.pdf. The PDF describes 22 icon triplets for the major arcana, placed on the upper-left corner of the correct major card. Acceptance: every major arcana card rendered by the app shows the correct icon triplet consistently across board, hand, and card-zone surfaces; minor arcana rendering is unchanged; and the implementation uses maintainable source data and assets instead of hard-coded view-only exceptions.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon triplets across shared card metadata, DOM card views, Three.js textures, and verification.

title: Render major arcana icons on Three.js card textures
description: Add the same major arcana icon triplets to the primary Three.js board cards. Acceptance: generated card textures or overlay meshes include the upper-left triplet on major cards, texture loading and the r128 runtime gate keep working, icons are visible on desktop and mobile board views, selection and raycasting behavior is unchanged, and missing icon assets produce an explicit renderer error instead of a silent blank overlay.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Render major arcana icons in DOM card views
description: Render the major arcana icon triplets on 2D card surfaces such as hand cards, card-zone previews, and the CSS fallback board. Acceptance: major cards show the ordered triplet in the upper-left overlay, the overlay scales with card size without covering titles or important imagery, minor cards remain visually unchanged, and DOM rendering uses the shared card metadata and icon assets rather than duplicating per-card conditionals.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Create scalable Gnostica icon assets
description: Add the visual assets needed to render the Stickas major arcana icon triplets. Acceptance: icons are repository-native scalable assets or components suitable for both DOM and canvas/texture rendering, the asset strategy avoids depending on cropped PDF raster images unless rights and quality are explicitly accepted, each icon has a stable id matching the metadata contract, and the icons remain legible at small card-overlay sizes.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Extract major arcana icon triplet metadata
description: Create the browser-free data contract for Gnostica major arcana icon triplets from the Stickas reference. Acceptance: all 22 major arcana card ids have exactly three ordered icon ids, card metadata exposes the triplet without coupling to Reagent or Three.js, tests fail if a major arcana card is missing icons or a minor arcana card gets major-only icons, and the source reference is noted in code or docs where maintainers will find it.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Verify major arcana icon rendering
description: Add focused verification for the major arcana icon overlay work. Acceptance: Clojure tests cover the metadata completeness and card lookup invariants, browser or smoke checks confirm at least one major card renders its icon triplet in both Three.js and CSS fallback paths, release compilation still succeeds with any new externs or assets, and the verification notes mention the Stickas PDF as the visual source.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Reduce move-panel duplication of browser-free move-selection state
description:
src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel.cljs duplicates state-machine decisions from src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc, including composite/sword major active-action functions and large per-power control case tables. This weakens cohesion because the UI infers the active action phase from :power and :major-actions instead of rendering explicit control metadata from the browser-free move-selection/app-state view model. Any future change to ordered major staging must be updated in multiple places.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move active action/control metadata into gnostica.move-selection or the app-state move-panel view model.
- Keep gnostica.ui.move-panel mostly declarative over view-model control groups.
- Remove duplicate composite-active-action-power and sword-major-active-action-power logic from the UI namespace.
- Add focused tests that view-model control groups match staged powers for representative composite, Sword-major, Sun, and World flows.
close_reason: Implemented move-panel control groups in browser-free move selection, refactored UI rendering, and added focused view-model tests.

title: Track browser move-selection gaps for implemented major powers
description: Pure browser-free facades now exist for Fool, High Priestess, Judgement, World, and the ordered multi-action/composite major flows, but the browser move-selection power list only exposes :cup, :rod, :disc, :sun, :sword, :hierophant, :hermit, and :devil. Some major cards are usable only as single suit subsets, and World/draw-major powers are unavailable from the browser despite implemented pure transitions. Acceptance: either add a clear browser-support epic with child tickets for these remaining staged controls or wire the missing flows directly; keep explicit unavailable results for powers still intentionally unwired so implemented pure rules do not disappear from product planning.
close_reason: Tracked remaining browser support under bd-ydt with child tickets for draw majors, ordered composite suit majors, Sword-major composites, World delegation, and power-choice/unavailable-state audit.

title: Clarify Wheel draw-pile Cup territory value constraints
description: docs/rules.txt:152-155 says Wheel can create a territory by drawing the first draw-pile card instead of using a hand card, and explicitly notes this can produce a major arcana territory. features/cups_moves.feature:48-56 only covers a draw-pile spot card, while test/gnostica/feature_world.clj:765-783 fixes that card to coins2. Clarify whether Wheel fully bypasses the normal Cup one-point hand-card restriction for any drawn card value, then add Cucumber coverage for a royalty or major draw-pile result or tighten the local rules text.
close_reason: Wheel draw-pile Cup Cucumber coverage now includes a major territory result.

title: Split production move-selection facade into focused modules
description: src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc is still 5,730 lines even after introducing focused controls, targets, commands, registry, confirmation, and context namespaces. That makes fresh review hard: source/power selection, staged requirements, option derivation, previews, target validation probes, Fool/World/high-variance major flows, and mutation summaries all sit in one file with broad private coupling. Existing bd-26kz covers splitting large tests, but the production facade remains a separate maintainability risk. Acceptance: extract at least the largest cohesive clusters (for example staged requirement/progress, Fool/World staging, preview view models, and option derivation glue) behind small internal namespaces; keep the public gnostica.move-selection API stable; run clojure -M:test plus release/smoke if browser-facing behavior changes; update MIND_MAP/README references if ownership boundaries move.
close_reason: Split move-selection facade internals into options, ribbon, and preview namespaces; tests, release, and smoke pass.

title: Split app.cljs browser shell into focused view modules
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: src/main/gnostica/app.cljs is 1263 lines and acts as re-frame registration, CSS fallback board renderer, shared card renderer, card zones, territory panel, move panel, help dialogs, keyboard shortcuts, startup, and smoke init hook. The first 300 lines are event/sub registration, while later sections contain board rendering, move controls, dialogs, global key handling, and init logic. Since domain state is already delegated to app_state and 3D rendering to three_board, this namespace is carrying too many browser responsibilities.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move board fallback/card-zone/territory-panel/move-panel/help-dialog views into focused UI namespaces.
- Keep re-frame event/sub registration either in a thin wiring namespace or colocated with clear feature boundaries.
- Keep gnostica.app as the browser shell/startup entry point.
- Preserve existing subscriptions/events and smoke-visible DOM attributes.
close_reason: done

title: Add gameplay move selection controls
description:
Goal: build the user interface flow that lets a player choose and confirm legal gameplay moves once the pure gameplay state and rule transitions are available.

Requirements:
- Add a move-selection mode to the app UI that is driven by shared gameplay state rather than renderer-only board state.
- Let the current player choose the move source, such as activating a board territory, playing a hand card, drawing cards, orienting a piece, or placing an initial small piece when they have no pieces on the board.
- Guide the player through required targets and parameters for the selected move: card, piece/minion, target space or piece, orientation, hand-card choice, draw count, and confirmation/cancel states as applicable.
- Surface only currently legal choices when the rule engine can provide them, and show clear disabled/error states for rejected transitions.
- Keep Three.js board selection, CSS fallback selection, territory panel, and future hand/player panels synchronized through re-frame events/subscriptions.
- Make the flow keyboard and pointer accessible enough for repeated gameplay, with predictable focus and no reliance on hover-only controls.
- Preserve deterministic, testable boundaries: UI events should call pure transition helpers and render their explicit success/error results.

Acceptance criteria:
- The UI has a clear current-player move picker and staged selection controls for source, target, options, confirm, and cancel.
- Selecting board cards and pieces feeds the active move flow instead of only updating the inspection panel.
- Invalid or incomplete selections cannot silently mutate state; errors are visible and recoverable.
- Tests cover the re-frame event/subscription state machine where practical, and manual smoke notes cover board/card/piece selection integration.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if new UI state namespaces, commands, or gameplay verification steps are added.

Implementation notes:
- This should follow the shared gameplay state/player setup/rule-transition work closely enough that the UI can consume authoritative move legality instead of duplicating rules.
- Prefer small reusable controls for move source selection, target prompts, orientation selection, and confirmation actions.
close_reason: Implemented move-selection UI flow with app-state tests, docs, release build, and smoke verification.

title: Allow keyboard-only first-piece placement targeting
description:
Let a no-piece current player place their first small piece without dragging. The player should be able to start first-piece placement from the existing Place first piece source, move a visible keyboard highlight across legal empty territories and wastelands with arrow keys, cancel the staged keyboard placement, and confirm it through the same pending move/initial-placement flow used by drag and Detailed entry.

Acceptance:
- When Place first piece is available, a keyboard-only path can enter placement targeting without pointer dragging.
- Arrow keys move a visible highlighted target among legal empty territories and wastelands using stable board indexes and wasteland coordinates; disabled/occupied/void targets are not selectable.
- The highlighted target is reflected in board preview, pending tray, and move-selection params without mutating :game before confirmation.
- Keyboard controls can cancel the pending placement and can confirm only when target plus orientation are complete, routing through apply-initial-placement.
- Existing orientation hotkeys continue to work for pending first-piece orientation, and existing 3D camera arrow/WASD panning still works when keyboard placement targeting is not active.
- Works in Three.js and CSS fallback where practical, with focused app-state/gesture coverage and smoke coverage for the keyboard-only path.
close_reason: done

title: Recompress MIND_MAP after recent expansions
description:
MIND_MAP.md line 3 asks agents to keep the map compact (20-50 nodes typical) and line-oriented, but the file now has 53 nodes and several very large single-line nodes, especially [24], [29], [37], [38], [46], and [47]. This makes grep output and review diffs hard to use. A previous compaction ticket was closed before the latest expansions, so this is current drift rather than an open duplicate.

Acceptance: consolidate, split, or rebalance nodes so the map stays within the stated compact target or deliberately revise the target; keep [6-10] as practical anchors, preserve [1-5], and reduce extreme node lengths while retaining useful cross-links.
close_reason: Recompressed MIND_MAP.md to 50 nodes and shortened oversized nodes.

title: Add local lobby for player setup before game start
description:
Add a first-screen local lobby flow before creating the game. This is scoped to the current single-browser app: players join locally, enter a display name, choose an available player colour, and then start the game. Networked multiplayer sync remains out of scope unless a separate ticket adds it.

Acceptance criteria:
- App initialization can start in a lobby state without immediately creating :game.
- Lobby UI lets two to six players join, edit their display names, choose unique colours from gnostica.pieces/players metadata, remove joined players before start, and start only when setup is valid.
- Starting the game calls the existing gnostica.game-state/create-game path with the selected player ids/names while preserving deterministic shuffle injection through the app event/cofx boundary.
- Existing demo/smoke init options can still bypass or prefill the lobby so current verification flows remain deterministic.
- Header, board, card-zone, territory, and move-panel views handle the pre-game lobby state without nil-game errors.
- Add app-state and UI-facing tests for lobby validation, duplicate colour rejection, player count limits, name persistence, start-game initialization, and cancellation/removal behavior.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the startup flow, app-db shape, or smoke workflow changes.
close_reason: Implemented in the current codebase and verified with clojure -M:test.

title: Align World copied-major command contracts with copied-power selection
description: The browser World flow asks the player to choose a copied power for copied major cards, but the public command contract only accepts suit keywords in :copied-power/:power. Evidence: valid-command? :world rejects a command with :copied-power :empress, while the same Empress-copy command validates when the selector is omitted. UI-generated commands currently omit :copied-power for full-major copies, but this makes the public browser-free contract asymmetric and confusing for callers. Relevant code: src/main/gnostica/game_state/command_contracts.cljc world-command-entries restricts selectors to SuitPower; src/main/gnostica/move_selection/commands.cljc only adds :copied-power for copied suit powers; app-state World tests show copied Empress/Death commands without the selected full-major power. Acceptance: either allow copied full-major power keywords and include them consistently in World commands, or document/test that :copied-power is suit-only and full-major selection is encoded solely by child payload shape.
close_reason: Allowed explicit World copied-power selectors for full-major copies, emitted them from browser commands, validated selected payload shapes, and covered pure/app-state command paths.

title: Split lobby app-state into setup, bidding, and view-model modules
description:
Fresh-eye review: src/main/gnostica/app_state/lobby.cljc has grown into an 803-line namespace that handles several different responsibilities at once.

Evidence:
- Lines 8-189 normalize players/controllers, target scores, lobby creation, and validation.
- Lines 213-331 mutate lobby seats, names, colors, and target score.
- Lines 333-400 define starting-bid helper state and redraw completion rules.
- Lines 402-628 start official bidding, select/reveal bid cards, manage redraw cards, confirm/cancel bidding, and start casual games.
- Lines 630-803 build UI view-model data for colors, bid-card summaries, redraw order, history, player rows, and lobby controls.

Problem: pure lobby-seat editing, official bid staging, game-start app-db transitions, and UI view-model composition are separate concepts but are coupled through private helpers in one file. A change to official bidding or view text has to load and reason about seat validation and game initialization paths too.

Acceptance criteria:
- Split lobby seat normalization/validation, starting-bid workflow state, game-start app-db transitions, and lobby view-model composition into focused namespaces under src/main/gnostica/app_state/ while preserving the public gnostica.app-state facade.
- Keep starting-bid behavior browser-free and still routed through gnostica.game-state/resolve-starting-bid-rounds and apply-starting-bids.
- Keep local-controller metadata stripping and demo-board-piece start options in the start-game boundary.
- Add or preserve tests that cover casual start, official bid ties/redraws, validation errors, and lobby-view derivation after the split.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md node [24] if the ownership map changes.
close_reason: Split lobby app-state into focused setup, bidding, start, and view-model namespaces; preserved facade and verification passed.

title: Harden create-game validation for player and deck inputs
description:
Problem: `gnostica.game-state/create-game` validates player count, missing ids, duplicate ids, and minimum deck size, but it accepts player ids with no known piece/player metadata and accepts any deck-like collection with at least nine entries. That leaves the authoritative state boundary open to malformed states before Malli/schema work exists. It also exposes a rule mismatch: setup claims two to six players, while `gnostica.pieces/players` currently defines only four color/player records.

Evidence:
- `normalize-player` merges optional `pieces/players-by-id` data and then the caller supplied player spec, so unknown ids can enter state with incomplete color metadata.
- `validate-deck` checks only `(count deck) >= board/board-card-count`, not uniqueness or card shape.
- Existing tests reject too few or too many players but do not cover unknown ids, five/six-player metadata, duplicate cards, or malformed deck entries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide and enforce whether player ids must be from known player metadata or whether custom player metadata is required for unknown ids.
- Two-through-six-player setup either has complete player/color metadata for every legal player count or returns structured validation errors explaining the unsupported players.
- Deck validation catches duplicate card ids and malformed/missing card data needed by setup and rendering.
- Validation failures use the existing explicit result map shape with stable error codes and useful `:data`.
- Tests cover unknown player ids, five/six-player setup behavior, duplicate deck entries, malformed cards, and the existing happy path.
close_reason: Hardened create-game player/deck validation, added six player metadata, expanded tests, and documented the stricter boundary.

title: Drive browser state from the shared game-state contract
description:
Problem: README.md and AGENTS.md identify `gnostica.game-state/create-game` as the authoritative browser-free gameplay state boundary, but `gnostica.app/::initialize` still builds a separate renderer-oriented state with `board/initial-board`, `pieces/initial-pieces`, and `:selected-board-index`. That creates a second setup path that bypasses explicit success/error results, player metadata, turn order, draw/discard state, setup/bid metadata, and future rule transitions.

Evidence:
- `src/main/gnostica/app.cljs` initializes top-level `:board` and `:pieces` directly.
- `src/main/gnostica/game_state.cljc` already provides deterministic `create-game` results intended for re-frame and tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser initialization creates and stores an authoritative game state through `gnostica.game-state/create-game` for the default/demo players, or renders an explicit setup error if creation fails.
- Board, piece, selected territory, and current-player subscriptions derive from the shared game state rather than parallel renderer-only top-level state.
- Any demo-only visible piece fixtures are either represented through the gameplay state contract or isolated and named as non-game fixtures.
- Focused tests cover the initialization path and at least one selection/update path against the new state shape.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the app-db shape or verification workflow changes.
close_reason: Browser app initialization now stores the shared create-game state under :game, derives board/piece/current-player subscriptions from it, covers the app-db path with focused tests, and updates README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP.

title: Clarify code-spelunker cs usage in AGENTS
description: AGENTS.md says to try the cs command in preference to grep or cat when exploring. Local cs is code-spelunker search, not a file reader; a command like cs README.md searches for that term and does not display README.md content. The instruction currently conflates search and exact file reading, which can waste time or cause agents to skip line-numbered review. Acceptance: AGENTS.md gives one or two concrete cs search examples, says it is for search/exploration, and clarifies that exact file review should use normal line-numbered reads when needed.
close_reason: Clarified AGENTS.md cs search usage with examples and line-numbered read guidance; updated MIND_MAP workflow note.

title: Close command schemas over unknown fields
description: gnostica.game-state.command-schema uses open Malli maps for public command contracts, so validate-command accepts typoed or unrelated keys. Repro: (game-state/valid-command? :draw {:player-id :rose :draw-count 1 :bogus 42}) returns true, and a valid Cup command with :bogus also returns true. This weakens the 'explicit command contract' boundary and can hide browser command-builder mistakes, especially in high-variance major, World, Fool, Disc/Sword replacement, and ordered action payloads. Acceptance: decide which maps should be closed versus extension-friendly, enumerate the actual top-level and nested action keys used by public facades, reject unexpected keys with structured explain-command output, and add regression tests for typoed top-level fields plus nested major/action payload fields while preserving legitimate World/Fool delegated commands.
close_reason: Implemented closed command schemas and regression tests

title: Players with no board pieces can end turn before mandatory placement
description: docs/rules.txt says a player with no pieces on the board may only put a small piece on any empty territory or wasteland. The shared end-turn facade in src/main/gnostica/game_state/core.cljc does not enforce that no-piece restriction, and header state exposes end turn whenever the game is active and current player exists. Reproduced a fresh game with no pieces where gnostica.game-state/end-turn for :rose succeeded and advanced to :indigo. Acceptance: a current player with no active board or wasteland pieces must perform apply-initial-placement before ending the turn, browser controls should reflect that restriction, and regression tests cover the fresh-game case plus normal end-turn after placement.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Make clojure -M:test discover or validate all test namespaces
description: test/gnostica/test_runner.clj manually requires and passes each test namespace to run-tests at lines 1-38. The current list matches the repository today, but adding a new *_test.clj file without editing this list would leave those tests out of clojure -M:test with no warning. Acceptance: either discover test namespaces from test/gnostica automatically, or keep an explicit list with a self-check that fails when a matching test namespace is not included; smoke-only helper namespaces can stay excluded by rule; add regression coverage or a runner assertion for the omission case.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic test namespace discovery for clojure -M:test and added runner discovery regression coverage.

title: Make High Priestess browser redraw staging sequential
description:
Browser staging validates both High Priestess redraw passes against the original post-source hand instead of the hand after prior staged passes. high-priestess-hand-card-options, high-priestess-valid-discard-card-ids, and the draw-count options do not simulate pass 1 before pass 2. The UI can stage pass 1 discard cups2 draw 0 and pass 2 discard cups2 draw 0 as :confirm; apply-high-priestess-move then rejects with :invalid-discard-cards. Conversely, pass 2 cannot select cards drawn in pass 1.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each redraw pass derives discard and draw options from the state after source cost and all earlier staged redraw passes.
- The same card cannot be discarded after it already left the hand unless it has actually been redrawn.
- Pass 2 support for newly drawn cards is implemented, or the limitation is explicitly represented in the staging UX and command validation.
- App-state tests cover duplicate discard prevention and the chosen behavior for cards drawn during pass 1.
close_reason: Implemented sequential High Priestess redraw staging with app-state coverage.

title: Add feature scenarios for Cup and Sword rule regressions
description:
The feature suite covers setup, Rod, Disc, draw majors, manipulation majors, and endgame, while core Cup and Sword behavior is covered mostly by unit/app-state tests. The missing feature layer made it easier for a Cup target-range regression to survive: there is no Gherkin scenario that says a Cup can only affect the minion target space. Sword has broad unit coverage but no feature-level rule narrative for attack/replacement/destruction flows.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add features/cups_moves.feature covering target range, enemy-piece creation, wasteland territory creation, full territory rejection, and key variants such as Wheel or Empress.
- Add features/swords_moves.feature covering piece attack, territory attack, replacement rejection, destruction/void handling, and at least one Tower or Death scenario.
- Step definitions reuse test/gnostica/feature_world.clj helpers and preserve Malli validation checks.
- clojure -M:test includes the new scenarios.
close_reason: Added Cup and Sword Gherkin feature scenarios covering target range, variants, attacks, replacements, destruction, void handling, and Tower discard-pile replacement.

title: Initial piece drag does not highlight placement destinations
description:
Actual: When the current player has no pieces and drags the first small piece for initial placement, the board does not highlight any legal destination.

Expected: While dragging the first piece, legal empty territories and legal wasteland destinations should be highlighted using the same move-legal-target descriptors used by Detailed entry, Three.js, and the CSS fallback.

Repro notes: Start a normal local game from the lobby so the current player has no pieces on the board. Drag the first/initial placement piece toward the board. No destination highlight appears before drop.

Scope: Direct manipulation initial placement highlighting. Check both Three.js and CSS fallback paths, and keep the pending tray/Detailed entry fallback behavior intact.

Related docs: README.md Gameplay Move Selection, AGENTS.md move-selection guidance, MIND_MAP.md nodes [14], [24], [37], [43], and [51].
close_reason: Fixed source-only initial-placement stash drags so they keep all empty territory and wasteland legal-target descriptors active for board highlighting; added app-state regression coverage and verified test, release, and smoke.

title: Replace regex source parsing in move-panel registry coverage
description:
Fresh-eye review found a brittle test shortcut in renderer coverage.

Evidence:
- test/gnostica/app_state/registry_test.clj:33-42 slurps src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel/controls.cljs and uses a regex to parse the private def control-renderers map.
- src/main/gnostica/ui/move_panel/controls.cljs:470-499 already has the authoritative renderer map, but it is private and only reachable to tests by source-text parsing.

Problem: formatting changes, macro rewrites, or small refactors can break coverage even when behavior is unchanged, or let behavior drift if the regex no longer matches the real renderer map. This also works against the registry-driven move-panel design.

Acceptance:
- Expose a small renderer-key set or testable registry view from gnostica.ui.move-panel.controls, or move renderer keys into shared data consumed by both UI and tests.
- Replace source-text regex parsing in registry_test with direct data/API coverage.
- Keep production rendering behavior unchanged and preserve the test that all registry renderer keys have UI renderers.
close_reason: done

title: Trim gnostica.move-selection back to a real facade
description: Fresh-eye review found src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc still acting as much more than a public facade. Evidence: lines 19-164 re-export registry, option, and state internals; lines 1027-1246 simulate High Priestess redraw state; lines 1284-1335 decide source availability and user-facing reasons; lines 1337-1646 rebuild helper contexts, expose view queries, and own prompt text; lines 1648 onward still owns direct selection/update handlers. Problem: the namespace has facade, rule derivation, UI copy, command probing, and app-db mutation responsibilities in one 3247-line file, so future move work still has a large merge and review surface after the recent extraction. Acceptance: keep the public gnostica.move-selection API stable, move remaining source availability, prompt text, redraw simulation, target option derivation, and selection update handlers into focused namespaces, leave this file mostly as compatibility facade and thin wrappers, and add tests around any moved boundary that was previously private.
notes: Progress 2026-06-14: extracted source availability, prompt text, High Priestess redraw staging, and target option derivation from gnostica.move-selection into focused move_selection namespaces. The public facade wrappers remain stable and clojure -M:lint plus clojure -M:test pass. Remaining acceptance work: move the large direct select/set update handler block out of the facade.
close_reason: Completed: move-selection facade now delegates source availability, prompt/redraw/targeting, target options, and selection update handlers to focused namespaces with lint and tests green.

title: Return pieces to stash when territory relocation leaves them in the void
description: Rod territory pushes and Hermit territory moves leave source-coordinate pieces behind by converting pieces on the moved territory to {:space {:kind :wasteland :row ... :col ...}} before moving the card. Unlike Sword territory destruction, these paths do not re-check whether that old coordinate is still a wasteland after the card moves. In a disconnected or gapped board, moving a territory from [0,0] to a wasteland at [0,2] adjacent to another territory can leave the old [0,0] coordinate in the void while the piece remains active. Acceptance: after any territory relocation, return pieces at coordinates that are no longer a territory or current wasteland to their owner stashes, keep stash mirrors reconciled, and cover both Rod territory push and Hermit territory move.
close_reason: done

title: Change board ground to a velvet color
description:
Goal: make the surface beneath the territory cards feel more like a velvet play mat instead of a neutral ground.

Requirements:
- Change the 3D board ground/table surface under the cards to a velvet-toned color.
- Preserve card readability, piece contrast, lighting balance, and existing board spacing.
- Keep the change visual-only: do not alter board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.
- Consider whether the CSS fallback board background should use a matching velvet color for consistency.
- Avoid adding image or texture dependencies unless a flat material cannot produce the desired result.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D board renders with a clearly velvet-colored ground beneath the cards.
- Cards and Icehouse pieces remain legible against the new surface.
- Existing tests and release compilation still pass.
- A smoke or screenshot-based check covers the changed board background if practical.
close_reason: Implemented burgundy velvet 3D and fallback board ground with smoke coverage.

title: Render and target all pieces in unbounded spaces
description: Frontend renderers still cap visible occupants at three via src/main/gnostica/board_layout.cljc:134 and src/main/gnostica/ui/board.cljs:329 even though Empress and Emperor style unbounded powers can legally create spaces with more than three pieces. Fourth and later pieces remain in game state but are invisible on both Three.js and CSS fallback boards, and likely unavailable as direct visual targets. Acceptance: render or summarize overflow occupants in both board renderers, keep every occupant selectable from the board or an adjacent UI, and add a regression fixture/test for a more-than-three-piece space.
close_reason: Rendered all overflow board pieces in Three.js and CSS fallback, kept all occupants in legal-target descriptors, added overflow fixture/regression coverage, and verified clojure -M:test, clojure -M:release, and clojure -M:smoke.

title: Sun created-target shortcuts skip Cup targeting validation
description: docs/rules.txt makes Sun a Cup followed by Disc, so same-created-target shortcuts still need the Cup target to be in the acting minion target space. In src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc, apply-sun-created-piece-shortcut and apply-sun-created-territory-shortcut validate resources and destination shape but never check the target coordinate against the minion. Reproduced apply-sun-move succeeding with a minion at board index 0 facing east while creating a medium piece at board index 8, and also creating a royalty territory at wasteland row 3 col 2. Acceptance: both Sun shortcut paths reuse the same Cup target validation as normal Cup, add regression tests for out-of-range territory and wasteland shortcuts, and keep valid shortcut examples passing.
close_reason: Duplicate of already-open quality tickets bd-1y9, bd-3or, bd-3gn, bd-svw, and bd-3d8 respectively.

title: Use board cell indexes instead of vector positions in app-state and move-selection
description: Fresh-eye review found that app-state and move-selection often treat :board as a positional vector with get/contains? even though gameplay cells carry stable :index values. Sword territory destruction removes cells and leaves index gaps, so selecting board index 5 after index 4 is destroyed returns the cell with :index 6; higher valid indexes can be rejected entirely. Affects selected-board-cell/select-board-card, move-selection target/source lookup, piece coordinate labels, and option derivation. Acceptance: centralize board-index lookup by :index for app-state subscriptions, move-selection, and UI labels; add coverage for selecting/targeting a surviving territory after a territory is destroyed and after creating a new territory in a gapped board.
close_reason: Fixed app-state and move-selection board lookups to use stable cell indexes with gapped-board coverage

title: Render pyramid piece edges with black outlines
description:
Goal: make Icehouse pyramid pieces easier to read on tarot cards by rendering visible black edge lines around each pyramid.

Requirements:
- Add a visual edge treatment to 3D pyramid meshes so their silhouettes and side-face boundaries remain visible against card art.
- Cover upright pieces and cardinal/lying pieces without changing existing piece position, orientation, or selection behavior.
- Prefer a Three.js-native approach such as edge geometry/line segments or an equivalent overlay that works with the current CDN r128 global build.
- Keep the CSS fallback behavior intact; add a matching fallback outline only if needed for visual consistency.
- Update externs if new Three.js APIs are used by advanced compilation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pyramids render with visible black edge lines in the 3D board view.
- Existing piece orientation and height tests still pass, and release compilation succeeds.
- A smoke or focused rendering check covers at least one selected viewport where edge outlines are expected to be present.
close_reason: done

title: Fail feature tests when parsed scenarios or steps are empty
description: The gameplay feature test only asserts that at least one .feature file exists, then calls assert-results-pass for each parsed result. feature-runner/assert-results-pass performs one assertion per result but returns true for an empty result collection, and run-scenario returns success for a scenario with no steps. A parser regression, empty feature file, misplaced Scenario heading, or accidentally blank scenario could therefore be reported as a passing test run with no scenario assertions for that file. Acceptance: gameplay feature tests assert each feature file expands to at least one scenario, and either the parser or runner rejects scenarios with zero executable steps; add regression coverage for empty feature files and empty scenarios so this cannot become a vacuous pass.
close_reason: Implemented empty feature and empty scenario guardrails with regression tests

title: Make World and Fool command-schema tests validate delegated command shapes
description: The representative command-schema tests for World and Fool can pass without proving the delegated command fields are meaningful. WorldCommand only requires :copied-board-index and Malli map schemas allow unrelated extra keys, so the current test World :target/:orientation data is accepted because it is ignored; invalid delegated data like :target {:kind :nonsense} still validates. FoolReveal accepts :command and :play-command as opaque :map, so a reveal play command with nonsense target shape also validates. This makes those tests partly vacuous for the high-variance command contracts they are meant to cover. Acceptance: model World delegated copied-power command fields and Fool revealed play-command shapes by reusing the appropriate child schemas or add explicit documented boundaries where semantic validation is deferred; add negative tests showing malformed delegated World/Fool commands fail the command contract when they are supposed to be machine-checkable.
close_reason: Implemented delegated World and Fool command-schema validation

title: Disable Activate territory when only wasteland pieces exist
description: A player can legally have only a wasteland piece, for example after initial placement to wasteland. In that state app-state/move-source-options reports :activate-territory as enabled because source-unavailable-reason only checks that some owned piece exists, but move-source-board-options is empty because no owned piece occupies a territory. Selecting the source leaves the move panel in a source-territory dead end with no valid source choices; direct piece selection can even stage {:source-board-index nil :piece-id ...}. Repro: initialize with no demo pieces, place Rose small at wasteland row 0 col 3, clear consumed turn metadata or advance back to Rose, then compare move-source-options and move-source-board-options. Acceptance: Activate territory is disabled or explains that no occupied territory source is available when the current player has only wasteland pieces; selecting disabled/stale activation cannot stage nil source-board-index; add app-state coverage for only-wasteland-piece source availability.
close_reason: Disabled Activate territory when the current player lacks an owned territory piece, rejected stale wasteland piece selection without staging nil source indexes, and added app-state regression coverage. Verified clojure -M:test and clojure -M:lint.

title: Enforce Cup target range for territory and enemy-piece creation
description:
Fresh review found Cup targeting is split. src/main/gnostica/game_state/cup.cljc checks cup-targetable-coordinate? for wasteland territory creation, but place-small-piece and create-enemy-small-piece do not check the acting minion target coordinate. Browser staging mirrors the gap: cup-target-piece? only checks enemy/territory shape and move-target-board-options falls through to every board cell for Cup and Sun Cup. A hand-card Cup through a minion on board index 0 facing east can create a small piece or enemy small at board index 8. This violates docs/rules.txt target-space rules for minions and Cups.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pure Cup rejects out-of-range territory and enemy-piece targets with structured errors.
- Browser options/filtering use the same target-coordinate rule for plain Cup, Sun Cup, Empress/Temperance/Lovers Cup actions, and World-copied Cup.
- Tests cover out-of-range pure commands and browser staging so illegal distant targets cannot reach confirm.
close_reason: Enforced Cup target-space validation in pure gameplay and browser staging, with regression coverage.

title: Update README direct move-entry spec wording
description: README.md still says docs/direct_move_entry_spec.md provides command-shape examples for future gesture work. AGENTS.md, MIND_MAP.md, the gesture-intent implementation, tests, and smoke coverage now treat that file as the active interaction contract for direct manipulation. Reword README.md to match the current status and avoid implying direct move entry is only future work.
close_reason: README now describes docs/direct_move_entry_spec.md as the active direct-manipulation interaction contract.

title: Mind map node [5] references a missing linked gist
description: MIND_MAP.md node [5] says the methodology includes a bootstrap prompt as a linked gist, but the node and surrounding file do not contain a gist URL or any actual link. This is small, but it weakens the self-documentation instructions because agents are told the reference exists when it does not. Evidence: MIND_MAP.md line 15. Add the link if it is still useful, or remove the phrase linked gist from the preserved generic node if that reference should not be part of this repository copy.
close_reason: Removed unsupported linked gist reference from MIND_MAP.md node [5].

description: README.md:41 and AGENTS.md:27 still imply drawing and discarding are entirely future pure gameplay transitions. But gnostica.game-state/apply-cup-move already removes a hand-source Cup card and app-state card-zone tests assert the discard pile updates. Clarify that the missing transition is the standalone draw/discard turn option, not every discard mutation, and keep README, AGENTS, and MIND_MAP wording aligned.

title: allow wasd style hotkey and arrow key movement of the camera/world
